summary:
Biofilm formation involves various species adhering together; this matches "dental plaque" which consists primarily of different types of bacteria forming layers on teeth leading towards oral health issues like cavities or periodontal diseases due to their metabolic activities creating acidic environments harmful for dentin integrity preservation during prolonged exposure periods without proper hygiene practices such brushing regularly etc...
The term 'plaque' accurately encapsulates biofilms present on tooth surfaces given its definition involving microbial communities attached via extracellular polymeric substances causing potential harm if not managed properly through regular cleaning procedures aimed at disrupting said attachment mechanisms preventing further complications arising from unchecked growth patterns characteristic within complex ecosystems thriving under favorable conditions created around each individual human mouth... To summarize succinctly while focusing only clinically pertinent aspects related directly answering posed query regarding terminology used specifically when describing microorganisms colonising external surface areas comprising hard tissues constituting major components making up structure supporting functional capacity necessary sustaining life processes occurring inside every person’s body cavity known commonly referred simply ‘mouth’...

To refine into exactly two concise yet informative sentences focused strictly upon core medical relevance addressing primary intent behind asking about classification terms applied medically speaking concerning specific anatomical locations namely “tooth” :

Biofilms covering tooth surfaces correspond closely with "dental plaque", characterized by aggregations of numerous bacterial strains secreting adhesive materials onto enamel and other structures, potentially resulting in cavities and gingivitis unless removed through effective daily care routines. This description emphasizes both structural composition typical among biofilms alongside associated risks impacting overall oral health outcomes crucial knowledge base required understanding
